title: Shapes of aromatic organic molecules; σ and π bonds
summary: 9701 A Level aromatic chemistry — benzene structure, delocalisation, and electrophilic substitution.
learningObjectives: Describe benzene structure and bond delocalisation.
Distinguish σ and π bonding in aromatic rings.
Explain stability of benzene vs cyclohexene.

simpleExplanation.title: Aromatic stability
simpleExplanation.summary: Benzene is planar with delocalised π electrons. Electrophilic substitution preserves aromaticity.
steps: Does not decolourise bromine water (unlike alkene).
E+ attack → arenium ion → substitution product.

sections.content: **Aromatic chemistry** (29.3) is tested with **mechanisms** (nitration, Friedel–Crafts). Never draw alternating C=C in benzene.
